biome_observations.debris_on_desk_surface: A white cylindrical object and wires are visible to the right of the yellow pot in [EARLIEST], [T-4], and [T-3].

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-4: Overall scene brightness slightly increased, revealing marginally more detail in the soil of both pots. No significant change in plant status.
temporal_deltas.t-4_to_t-3: Significant increase in scene brightness and clarity. The faint debris in P1 becomes clearly identifiable as dried plant strands. Eggshell fragments in the black pot become more distinct. Soil in P1 appears drier.
temporal_deltas.t-3_to_t-2: Complete loss of visual information; image is entirely black, indicating a camera malfunction, power outage, or intentional blackout.
temporal_deltas.t-2_to_t-1: Continued loss of visual information; image remains black with a very slight, almost imperceptible, greyish tint in some areas, still functionally a blackout.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Complete change of scene; the entire frame is now a uniform light grey, indicating the entire biome has been removed or the camera's view has been redirected.

anomalies: The presence of eggshell fragments in the black pot is an observed intervention not explicitly in the initial registry description.
The complete blackouts in images [T-2] and [T-1] represent significant data acquisition anomalies.
The complete absence of the entire biome and the uniform grey screen in the [CURRENT] image is a major anomaly, indicating a fundamental change in the monitoring setup or environment.
vision_report.narrative_description: The initial sequence of images ([EARLIEST], [T-4], [T-3]) paints a grim picture for the expected biome. Pot P1, designated for 'String of Nickels', shows only dry soil with what are clearly dead, dried plant remnants by [T-3]. The black pot, intended for 'Mexican Mint', 'Pothos', and 'Silver Guest', contains only soil and eggshell fragments, with no discernible living plant material or the 'White Rabbit' anchor. All registered plants are visually confirmed as lost or severely degraded to unidentifiable states. The eggshell fragments suggest a past attempt at intervention, but it has not yielded visible plant life. The monitoring sequence then suffers a complete loss of visual data in [T-2] and [T-1] due to blackouts, making any further plant assessment impossible for those periods. The final image ([CURRENT]) presents a uniform light grey field, indicating that the entire plant setup has been removed or the camera's view has been completely altered, resulting in a systemic loss of the biome from observation.
